title: viburnum opulus f. hydrangeoides
intro: Viburnum opulus f. hydrangeoides, also known as the Guelder rose or European cranberrybush, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its clusters of small white flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which are attributed to the presence of viburnin, a compound that has been shown to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Viburnum opulus f. hydrangeoides include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
